internal_anchors.details: Covered by test suite Section 3 (Internal Links). 12 tabs, all showTab() targets resolve.

tab_structure.details: All 12 tab-content divs have unique ids; all 183 showTab() calls target existing tabs (12 unique). nav_chain validation covered by test suite.

external_links.details: podaac.jpl.nasa.gov timed out on all three attempts over two retries (20s each) — 3 in-prose references affected (SEC 3.2 ks-detail, SEC 5.15 Exhibit B, SEC 5.15 ProdSpecDoc PDF). Likely JPL-side connectivity issue. 3 NASA/HeartMath redirects carried over from yesterday still land on generic pages (citation content effectively lost). NOAA 403s are benign bot-blocking (domains resolve).

data_prose_consistency.details: 70 WINs in wins.json (69 standard + WIN-058b mirror). Scorecard headline of 69 matches dome's count, not our total — consistent with yesterday's behaviour. Uncounted failures count (8) matches HTML rendered text.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 70 WINs have matching id="winNNN" anchors in docs/index.html.

progressive_disclosure.details: No empty TLDRs. Summary/tldr class counts balanced.

hardcoded_theme_colors.details: Two hardcoded light-theme backgrounds in sections.json. part6 Tesla patent container has existed for a while (image card). part1 is a formula-block style that should use var(--code-bg) or similar.

orphaned_output_files: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-165.json (no tracker entry; likely written by analyst before tracker append completed; next allocation at 165 will collide)
expansion_tracker.details: next_id > max_id (safe). EXP-052 gap is a known persistent issue. EXP-165.json exists on disk but has no tracker entry — classic write-before-commit race. next_id=165 means the next analyst run will attempt to allocate EXP-165 again and either overwrite this file or mis-index it.

workspace_only_files_at_risk.details: 9 analyst expansions + 21 curmudgeon reviews exist on workspace FUSE but are not in origin/main. They will persist only if workspace-sync commits them. The expansions list has grown by several since yesterday (now 158–165 + ISS-802-resolution). Several of the workspace-only reviews are from 'eg-*' and 'part*-roadmap-*' prefixes that historically tripped up the digest pipeline.

priority_queue_coupling.details: No section-new or category-proposal-writeup items in a non-integrated state. EXP-050 spurious-flag issue from yesterday is resolved (status is now 'integrated').

workspace_sync_skips.details: Zero direction violations. 18 mtime-guard skips are normal operation (git version is newer — decider committed directly).

digest_coverage.details: 6 unprocessed (under the 10-review escalation threshold, but note that 4 of the 6 are the broken-JSON files — once parse errors are fixed, they still need to flow into the digest). Digest file mtime 2026-04-14T21:26Z is older than newest review 2026-04-15T07:13Z — digest is stale and should be rebuilt with `node build-scripts/digest-reviews.js --workspace .` before the next decider triage.

files.error: Expected ',' or '}' after property value at line 32 col 114
Expected ',' or '}' after property value at line 35 col 224
Expected ',' or '}' after property value at line 65 col 503
Expected ',' or '}' after property value at line 91 col 818

broken_review_files.details: 4 curmudgeon reviews fail JSON parsing — their findings are invisible to the decider. All 4 are unescaped-quote patterns that fix-json-quotes.js can auto-repair. Every error message names the precise character offset.

dome_win_alignment.details: Dome site shows WIN-001 through WIN-069 (69 total). Our WIN-058b is an intentional mirror of dome's WIN-058 covering a distinct sub-claim (Bedrock Induction Ratio κ) — same pattern previously used for 057/058 family. No drift in coverage.

build_reproducibility.method: Cloned origin/main into /tmp/ghclone and ran `node build.js html`. Output diffed against origin/main's committed docs/index.html.
build_reproducibility.details: Build is fully reproducible. The 4765-line diff between workspace docs/index.html (just rebuilt from FUSE data) and origin/main's committed docs/index.html is explained by the workspace FUSE being ~6 commits behind origin (expected staleness). No drift when building from current origin data.

project_docs_mechanical.details: CLAUDE.md uses query-commands for every count (no hardcoded tallies). Schedule table matches Phase-1 expectations. No missing file-map entries observed in spot-check.

issues_found.description: 4 curmudgeon review JSON files have parse errors — findings silently dropped by digest pipeline.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Run: node build-scripts/fix-json-quotes.js monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/HOLISTIC-HOL-TONE.json monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-4.6.2.json monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-6.6.c2.json monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-068.c3.json — then regenerate digest.

issues_found.description: EXP-165.json exists on disk without a matching tracker entry. next_id is 165, so the next analyst allocation will collide with this file (either overwrite its contents or receive a duplicate EXP-165 allocation).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider should either: (a) append a tracker item for EXP-165 (target='SEC-1.9 / WIN-044 FSF citation pin (ISS-1009)', status='ready-for-integration', created_by='analyst', created_at='2026-04-14T20:14:05.599Z', bumping next_id to 166) or (b) rename the file to the next free EXP-NNN if the current analyst run intended to allocate a new slot. Root-cause: an analyst run wrote the output file but crashed or was killed before appending the tracker entry — worth a tinker investigation.

issues_found.description: 30 workspace-only output files (9 expansions + 21 reviews) not yet committed to origin/main. Backlog is growing vs yesterday (was 4, now 30).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Confirm workspace-sync runs successfully next cycle. If the backlog persists, investigate whether workspace-sync is silently failing on git push (check ownership classifications and mtime guards).

issues_found.description: pending-digest.json mtime (2026-04-14T21:26Z) is ~10 hours older than newest review file (2026-04-15T07:13Z). Decider triage may miss new findings until digest is regenerated.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Run `node build-scripts/digest-reviews.js --workspace .` (ideally after the 4 broken-JSON files above are repaired so the new digest doesn't re-record them as errors).

issues_found.description: podaac.jpl.nasa.gov unreachable on two retries (20s timeout each). Three in-prose references are affected.
issues_found.location: docs/index.html: SEC 3.2 ks-detail (line 1463), SEC 5.15 Exhibit B (line 3931), SEC 5.15 ProdSpecDoc PDF (line 3931)
issues_found.suggested_fix: Likely transient JPL connectivity. If persistent for >24h, swap to NASA's alternate PO.DAAC entry point or an archive.org mirror. Monitor for recurrence next run.

issues_found.description: Three known data-source redirects still land on generic hub pages (citation content effectively lost, carried from yesterday).
issues_found.location: nssdc.gsfc.nasa.gov earthfact.html → nasa.gov/nssdc/; earthobservatory.nasa.gov crepuscular-rays → science.nasa.gov/earth/earth-observatory/; heartmath.org gcms → heartmath.org/research/
issues_found.suggested_fix: Find replacement URLs (NASA moved several factsheet pages to science.nasa.gov/resource/*). If no direct equivalent exists, point to archive.org captures from 2023–2024.

issues_found.description: 2 hardcoded light-theme backgrounds in sections.json break dark-theme consistency.
issues_found.location: data/sections.json: part1.html (#f5f5f5 WGS84 formula background), part6.html (#fff Tesla patent image container)
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace with var(--code-bg) and var(--card-bg) respectively. The Tesla patent card likely wants white because of the transparent-background PNG — consider adding a dedicated .patent-card utility class rather than inline #fff.

issues_found.description: EXP-052 ID gap persists from prior reports (no tracker item, no output file).
issues_found.suggested_fix: One-shot tinker investigation: was EXP-052 ever allocated? If yes, recover from git history; if no, document the gap in tracker._meta as 'skipped' so future integrity runs can silence this.

issues_found.description: www.noaa.gov returns 403 to automated curl (bot-blocking, domain resolves fine).
issues_found.suggested_fix: False positive — no action required. Whitelist noaa.gov in the integrity agent's curl-check if this recurs.

issues_found.description: 70 tracker items have output_file=null without a no_output_file_reason note. Only 11 of 81 null-output-file items document the reason.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Back-fill no_output_file_reason on items where output_file is legitimately null (direct-integration items, template applications). Low urgency — informational only, does not trigger phantom-detection false positives.

issues_found.description: Numerous expansion output filenames include descriptive suffixes (e.g. EXP-004-sec31-32.json, EXP-010-sec48.json) that are not always referenced by the tracker's output_file field.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Cosmetic — all 248 files resolve to 247 unique EXP-NNN prefixes with only the EXP-165 genuine orphan listed above. Low priority.

summary: Overall WARN. Build reproducibility and test suite remain green (3327/3327). Two MAJOR findings this run: (1) four curmudgeon reviews have JSON parse errors and their findings are invisible to the decider — one-line auto-recovery with fix-json-quotes.js, (2) EXP-165.json was written without a tracker entry and will collide with the next EXP-NNN allocation. Moderate items are mostly carryovers (3 redirected data-source links, 2 hardcoded light-theme colors in sections.json, EXP-052 gap) plus three new issues (podaac.jpl.nasa.gov timeouts, stale digest, growing workspace-only backlog of 9 expansions + 21 reviews vs 4 yesterday). Priority-queue coupling, direction-guard skips, tab structure, data/prose consistency, progressive disclosure, discoverability infrastructure, and dome WIN alignment are all clean.
